summary: Drew DeVault provides a brief September 2020 status update focused primarily on SourceHut development progress. After completing operations work, he shifted focus to software development, particularly GraphQL APIs and OAuth 2.0 support. He notes progress on writable GraphQL APIs and beta milestone tickets. He also mentions growing adoption of the BARE message encoding format across multiple programming languages.
key_points: SourceHut development shifted from operations to software work, with focus on GraphQL APIs and OAuth 2.0
Beta milestone tickets are being systematically addressed, starting with meta.sr.ht improvements
The BARE encoding format reached nine implementations across languages including Python, Common Lisp, and PHP
money_quotes: I've been able to put a pin on operations work on SourceHut for the time being, and focus again on its software development.
We have a pretty decent spread of support among programming languages!
key_insight: SourceHut development is transitioning from operational maintenance to active feature development, with GraphQL APIs and OAuth 2.0 as the primary focus areas for the upcoming beta.
